# terrible_netapp_cloudmanager_na_cloudmanager_aws_fsx (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) The name of the CVO/Working Environment for AWS FSx to manage.
- `tenant_id` (String) The NetApp account ID that the File System will be associated with.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `aws_credentials_name` (String) The name of the AWS Credentials account name.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `endpoint_ip_address_range` (String) The endpoint IP address range.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `file_system_id` (String) The AWS file system ID to import to CloudManager. Required when import_file_system is 'True'
- `fsx_admin_password` (String) The admin password for Cloud Volumes ONTAP fsxadmin user.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `import_file_system` (Boolean) bool option to existing import AWS file system to CloudManager.
- `kms_key_id` (String) AWS encryption parameters. It is required if using aws encryption.
- `minimum_ssd_iops` (Number) Provisioned SSD IOPS.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `primary_subnet_id` (String) The subnet ID of the first node.
- `region` (String) The region where the working environment will be created.
- `route_table_ids` (String) The list of route table IDs that will be updated with the floating IPs.
- `secondary_subnet_id` (String) The subnet ID of the second node.
- `security_group_ids` (String) The IDs of the security groups for the working environment, multiple security groups can be provided separated by ','.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Whether the specified FSx in AWS should exist or not.
- `storage_capacity_size` (Number) volume size for the first data aggregate. For GB, the value can be [100 or 500]. For TB, the value can be [1,2,4,8,16].
- `storage_capacity_size_unit` (String) The unit for volume size.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `throughput_capacity` (Number) The capacity of the throughput.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `working_environment_id` (String) The ID of the AWS FSx working environment used for delete.
- `workspace_id` (String) The ID of the Cloud Manager workspace of working environment.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
